title: Veto rides hunch_merge_verdict; no dedicated hunch_veto MCP tool
context: The veto guard (block re-introducing a rejected alternative) needs an agent-facing self-check surface so a patch can be vetted before it is written. The deck proposed a new read-only MCP tool hunch_veto({diff}). But hunch_merge_verdict already replays a diff through buildCheckReport and renders the WHOLE CheckReport (src/mcp/server.ts). Touches invariant con_e04226bd05 (the MCP server must stay client-agnostic; no per-feature special-casing).
decision: Do NOT add a hunch_veto MCP tool. Put vetoes in CheckReport.vetoes (tier 4); they then surface through the existing hunch_merge_verdict for free, alongside hunch check and the CI guard. Expose hunch veto only as a CLI flag for the isolated rejected-alternative class (demo + debugging).
consequences: One CheckReport change lights up three surfaces (check, CI markdown, hunch_merge_verdict) with no per-surface work
MCP surface stays minimal and client-agnostic (con_e04226bd05) — fewer tools to keep from drifting
An agent self-checking a patch uses the same hunch_merge_verdict call it already makes before a PR
hunch veto CLI remains for running the rejected-alternative class in isolation
alternatives_rejected: Add a dedicated read-only hunch_veto({diff}) MCP tool (re-walks the same buildCheckReport path; adds a per-feature surface that violates the client-agnostic invariant)
Expose veto only via CLI with no agent-loop surface (agent could not self-check before writing)
